| ID | Name | Description | Source File | Domains |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| FIL-CH-200 | Mindset Is The Only Differentiator | "The only difference between 10K and 80K a month honestly is that mindset." Mindset determines whether tools, blueprints, and funnels actually produce results. | 01/02/01/transcription_mp4_01_How_to_achieve_a_Millionaire_s_Mindset.md | agency-building, mindset |
| FIL-CH-201 | Value Is Belief-Based | "The only thing that matters when it comes to pricing is how big is the problem that you solve and what do you believe it's worth?" Pricing is a function of self-belief, not market rates. | 01/02/01/transcription_mp4_01_How_to_achieve_a_Millionaire_s_Mindset.md | pricing, mindset |
| FIL-CH-202 | People Are Your Only Asset | "As an agency, your only asset is your people. You don't really have any assets aside from a couple laptops." Agency value is entirely in human capital. | 01/01/transcription_mp4_01_ACADEMY_-_Welcome_-.md | agency-building |
| FIL-CH-203 | Never Promise Results | "Never ever promise results to a client. You don't own Facebook. The only person that could do that would be Mark Zuckerberg." Integrity requires honest expectation-setting. | 03/03/transcription_mp4_01_Pricing_Structures.md | client-management, pricing |
| FIL-CH-204 | No Refunds Policy | "Do not give refunds. Put this in your contract. If a client is committing to a certain amount of time and you have set the right expectations, they shouldn't be allowed to bail out." | 03/03/transcription_mp4_01_Pricing_Structures.md | client-management, contracts |
| FIL-CH-205 | Don't Take Clients For Money | "Do not take on clients just because you need the money. I guarantee you 1000% that will always backfire on you." Only onboard clients that are a right fit. | 03/02/01/transcription_mp4_01_How_to_Audit_an_Account.md | client-acquisition, auditing |
| FIL-CH-206 | Competence Before Clients | Academy structured in 4 locked levels requiring competency tests because students were landing clients without knowing what a pixel was. "The whole thing felt really out of integrity." | 01/01/transcription_mp4_01_ACADEMY_-_Welcome_-.md | agency-building, training |
| FIL-CH-207 | Niche Expertise Over Breadth | Specializing in verticals reduces learning curves, builds leverage through case studies, and enables premium pricing. "Learning curves cost money and they cost time." | 03/01/transcription_mp4_01_ACADEMY_LEVEL_2.md | agency-building, niches |
| FIL-CH-208 | Protect Your Profit Margin | "It is not okay that you take all the money out of your business. You need to make sure you have a profit margin... that should be 20 to 30%." Profit margin enables reinvestment and survival. | 01/03/transcription_mp4_01_Getting_your_Foundations_ready.md | agency-building, finance |
| FIL-CH-209 | Lowball Pricing Imprisons You | Charging $55/hr means 90-hour work weeks to earn $20K. Premium pricing is not vanity â€” it's the minimum required to build a team and deliver quality. | 03/03/transcription_mp4_01_Pricing_Structures.md | pricing, agency-building |
| FIL-CH-210 | Pressure Creates Diamonds | "Being an empire is not a pretty sight. It takes a lot of pressure to create a diamond out of a rock." Challenges during agency building are normal and expected. | 01/01/transcription_mp4_01_ACADEMY_-_Welcome_-.md | mindset |
| FIL-CH-211 | Subconscious Alignment Required | Only 1-3% of people who consciously want financial independence actually achieve it because their subconscious is not aligned with their conscious goals. | 01/02/01/transcription_mp4_02_Two_Powerful_Exercises_for_Mindset.md | mindset |
| FIL-CH-212 | Money Goes To Those Who Treasure It | "Money goes to those who treasure it the most and who manage it the best." Financial management is prerequisite to financial growth. | 01/02/01/transcription_mp4_02_Two_Powerful_Exercises_for_Mindset.md | mindset, finance |
| FIL-CH-213 | Imposter Syndrome Is Uncertainty | Reframing imposter syndrome as "uncertainty syndrome" makes it actionable. "Confidence is repetitive courageous actions towards less uncertainty." | 01/02/01/transcription_mp4_03_Combatting_the_Imposter_Syndrome___.md | mindset |
| FIL-CH-214 | Think Like Agency Owner Not Freelancer | "If you're thinking I hate e-commerce, you're thinking like a freelancer. As an agency owner, you don't work with those clients â€” your team does." | 06/05/02/transcription_mp4_01_Agency_training.md | agency-building, mindset |
| FIL-CH-215 | Algorithm Is 70% Of Success | "Being really good in Facebook marketing is about 30% down to skills and knowledge and 70% letting the algorithm do its thing." Understanding the algorithm matters more than manual optimization. | 06/02/02/transcription_mp4_01_Thinking_like_the_Algorithm.md | fb-ads, fatc-overlap |

## L2: MENTAL MODELS (MM-CH-200 to MM-CH-224)

| ID | Name | Description | Source File | Domains |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| MM-CH-200 | Four Bottleneck Model | Agency scaling encounters 4 sequential bottlenecks: (1) Lead Generation, (2) Sales/Closing, (3) Delivery/HR (hardest), (4) Scaling/Systemization. | 01/01/transcription_mp4_01_ACADEMY_-_Welcome_-.md | agency-building |
| MM-CH-201 | Owned vs Leased Funnels | Two types of funnels: Owned (client keeps all assets) and Leased (agency owns assets, client leases access). Leased funnels lock clients into long-term contracts and attract investors. | 03/03/transcription_mp4_01_Pricing_Structures.md | pricing, funnels |
| MM-CH-202 | Two-Call Close Framework | Cold leads require a two-call process: Call 1 = discovery + rapport + request BM access; Call 2 = audit findings + pitch. Single-call closes only work for warm/referral leads. | 03/02/01/transcription_mp4_01_How_to_Audit_an_Account.md | client-acquisition, sales |
| MM-CH-203 | Working Backwards Model | Calculate required ad spend by working backwards from desired conversions through funnel steps using conversion rates at each stage. | 03/02/02/transcription_mp4_01_Calculating_Required_Ad_Spend.md | auditing, ad-spend |
| MM-CH-204 | Auction Process Trifecta | FB ad results determined by three factors: Advertiser Bid + Estimated Action (likelihood of user action) + Relevance & Ad Quality. Small advertisers can beat Coca-Cola with better relevance. | 06/02/02/transcription_mp4_01_Thinking_like_the_Algorithm.md | fb-ads, fatc-overlap |
| MM-CH-205 | CVR As Algorithm Factor | For conversion campaigns, conversion rate (CVR) is an additional factor. Facebook wants 50 conversions per ad set per week. If not hitting this, optimize for a step earlier in funnel (purchase -> add to cart -> view content). | 06/02/02/transcription_mp4_01_Thinking_like_the_Algorithm.md | fb-ads, ecom, fatc-overlap |
| MM-CH-206 | Minimum Viable Funnel Thinking | Always start with the simplest funnel (direct response -> product page -> DPA retargeting). If it works, you saved time. If not, now you have data to build up incrementally. | 06/03/02/transcription_mp4_01_MINIMUM_VIABLE_FUNNEL.md | ecom, funnels, fatc-overlap |
| MM-CH-207 | Price Point Determines Funnel Length | Cheaper products can use shorter funnels (impulse buying). Expensive products need longer funnels with more trust-building steps. Average order value, not unit price, is the real metric. | 06/03/02/transcription_mp4_01_MINIMUM_VIABLE_FUNNEL.md | ecom, funnels, fatc-overlap |
| MM-CH-208 | Problem-Agitate-Solve Copy Framework | Map out: Awesome (desired outcomes), Bad (pain points), Without/Even If (misconceptions/objections). Research via Amazon book descriptions, YouTube videos, and direct customer surveys. | 03/01/transcription_mp4_01_ACADEMY_LEVEL_2.md | copywriting, client-acquisition |
| MM-CH-209 | Vertical Lock-In Strategy | Build expertise in one vertical first, train team, build case studies, then add a second vertical. Never jump to a new niche before mastering the current one. | 06/05/02/transcription_mp4_01_Agency_training.md | agency-building, niches |
| MM-CH-210 | BM Access = Trust Accelerator | Requesting Business Manager access as analyst on first call doubles close rates. It's a "small yes" that builds trust â€” "they're handing you the keys to their house." | 04/01/05/transcription_mp4_01_The_Funnel_to_Land_Clients_Performance_Based.md | sales, client-acquisition |
| MM-CH-211 | Horizontal vs Vertical Scaling | Vertical = increase budget 16-20% every 2-3 days (slow, safe, CPA-focused). Horizontal = duplicate winning ad sets with variable changes (fast, aggressive, needs 200K+ audience). | 06/03/09/transcription_mp4_01_SCALING.md | fb-ads, ecom, fatc-overlap |
| MM-CH-212 | Referral Incentive Flywheel | Offer $1,200 off monthly invoice or 20% per referred client with 3-month sunset clause. Minimum invoice threshold ($3-5K) prevents low-value referrals. | 04/02/transcription_mp4_02_-_Referral_Based_Programs.md | client-acquisition, retention |

| MM-CH-405 | Dream 100 Pipeline | Write down 100 ideal clients/brands you want to work with. This is your targeting foundation. Without a clear target list, prospecting efforts have no direction and nothing will work. | 02__BONUS-_Landing_Big_Brands.md | prospecting, strategy |
| MM-CH-406 | Internal Hourly Rate Model | Calculate your true internal cost: time per service x cost per person. Then divide by your target cost-of-goods margin (e.g., 25%) to get the client price. This protects margins and enables scaling. | Building_Your_Service_Document.md | pricing, operations |
| MM-CH-407 | The Homeless Doctor Analogy | A homeless man who accurately describes your exact symptoms will earn more trust than credentialed doctors who dismiss you. Specificity of problem description trumps credentials every time. | Additional_Training.md | sales, positioning |
| MM-CH-408 | Know-Like-Trust Sequence | Before anyone buys from you, they need to know, like, and trust you. Your branding, content, and positioning are all engineered to move prospects through this sequence. | Overview.md | branding, sales |
| MM-CH-409 | Prospecting Methods Match Personality | Not all prospecting strategies work for all personalities. Cold calling might work for extroverts but fail for introverts. Choose strategies that leverage YOUR strengths rather than copying peers. | Additional_Training.md | prospecting, strategy |
| MM-CH-410 | The Category-Based Business | As you mature, you become a category-based business: everything you do revolves around one avatar, and you expand service SKUs within that category. This reduces trust-building overhead and enables upselling. | 1__NICHING_DOWN.md | agency-scaling, positioning |
| MM-CH-411 | Levels of Awareness (Schwartz Applied) | Prospects exist at different awareness levels: problem-aware, solution-aware, product-aware. Your funnel messaging must match where they are. ## CONSOLIDATED DNA EXTRACTION - RAFAEL MARKS (Sections 2-5 + Supplementary)

**Files Read: 25+** across Biblioteca dos Segredos, Treinamentos Rapidos, Bonus Desbloqueados, Hacks Mensais, Envelope Secreto PDFs, and Outlier Experience presentation.

---

### PHILOSOPHIES (Core beliefs, worldview)

- **FIL-RM-200:** "A diferenca do certo pro errado, ela e minima. Ta tudo nas entrelinhas." [source: transcription_mp4_A_NOVA_ONDA_DO_MARKETING_DIGITAL_MICRO_OFERTAS.md]

- **FIL-RM-201:** "Lucro e o nome do jogo. O nome do jogo e lucro." [source: transcription_mp4_MasterClass__2.md]

- **FIL-RM-202:** "A unica verdade sao os numeros. Essa e a verdade que tem quando a gente fala de funil, quando a gente fala de vendas na internet. A verdade esta nos dados, esta nos numeros." [source: transcription_mp4_MasterClass__1.md]

- **FIL-RM-203:** "A vida nao perdoa quando voce vai mais ou menos. Voce vai mais ou menos em qualquer coisa, voce nao tem o resultado que voce queria ter." [source: transcription_mp4_A_NOVA_ONDA_DO_MARKETING_DIGITAL_MICRO_OFERTAS.md]

- **FIL-RM-204:** "Todo produto e uma carta de vendas para o proximo. Disfarcado. Todo produto e um lancamento do proximo." [source: transcription_mp4_A_NOVA_ONDA_DO_MARKETING_DIGITAL_MICRO_OFERTAS.md]

- **FIL-RM-205:** "Voce pode ser a melhor semente do mundo, plantada em cimento. Vai dar nique. Vale mais o barco em que voce ta, do que quando voce sabe remar." [source: transcription_mp4_A_NOVA_ONDA_DO_MARKETING_DIGITAL_MICRO_OFERTAS.md]

- **FIL-RM-206:** "Quem fica pobre sabe tudo sobre o custo. Quem fica rico e a galera que esta viciada no quanto retorna." [source: transcription_mp4_A_NOVA_ONDA_DO_MARKETING_DIGITAL_MICRO_OFERTAS.md]

- **FIL-RM-207:** "Escala quando a gente fala de uma escala onde voce quer romper, romper significa rasgar na marra e na forca, e voce nao vai escalar se nao for desse jeito." [source: transcription_mp4_Mastermind_A_C_pula.md]

- **FIL-RM-208:** "Amadores focam no front-end. O que vem apos, vai te deixar rico." [source: transcription_pdf_EDIC_A_O_SECRETA_-_N2.md]

- **FIL-RM-209:** "O valor da coisa nao esta no tempo em que voce passa consumindo ela. O valor esta no resultado que aquilo gera quando voce implementa." [source: transcription_mp4_MasterClass__2.md]

- **FIL-RM-210:** "Voce anda com gente foda para poder ganhar dinheiro. E exatamente o contrario do que as pessoas pensam." [source: transcription_mp4_A_NOVA_ONDA_DO_MARKETING_DIGITAL_MICRO_OFERTAS.md]

- **FIL-RM-211:** "A venda nao acaba quando a pessoa comprou. A venda comeca ali. Depois que voce vender o produto, agora voce tem que vender para ela o consumo e a implementacao." [source: transcription_mp4_A_NOVA_ONDA_DO_MARKETING_DIGITAL_MICRO_OFERTAS.md]

- **FIL-RM-212:** "Nao te canses pra enriquecer, da de mao a tua propria sabedoria. Na maior parte das vezes, nao e sobre como. E sobre quem faz pra voce." [source: transcription_mp4_Close_Friends__3.md]

- **FIL-RM-213:** "Quando voce antecipa os sonhos com o dinheiro que ta saindo do seu funil, voce ta abrindo mao de um enriquecimento muito mais rapido. Quando a empresa ta gorda de caixa, voce multiplica muito rapido." [source: transcription_mp4_Mentalidade_Para_Enriquecer_Mais_R_pido_na_Internet.md]

- **FIL-RM-214:** "O diabo da na vez todas as estrategias boas pra quem vende mentira como se fosse verdade. E voce que e bom, integro, transforma, tem produtos bons, mas nao consegue vender bem, porque vende produto de verdade como se ele fosse uma mentira." [source: transcription_mp4_Dia_2.md]

---

### MENTAL MODELS (Thinking frameworks)

- **MM-RM-200:** "Regra do Melhor Primeiro Beijo: Sua mini-oferta precisa ser o melhor primeiro beijo que a pessoa ja deu em toda a vida dela. Quando voce da o melhor primeiro beijo, a pessoa nao precisa ser convencida, voce ja quer o segundo." [source: transcription_mp4_A_NOVA_ONDA_DO_MARKETING_DIGITAL_MICRO_OFERTAS.md]

- **MM-RM-201:** "Alternancia de Formato: Voce nao pode dar o produto na mesma experiencia do proximo. Analogia da balada - toda vez mesma boate, mesma camisa, mesmo beijo, ninguem volta. As experiencias mudam para o cliente acender." [source: transcription_mp4_A_NOVA_ONDA_DO_MARKETING_DIGITAL_MICRO_OFERTAS.md]

- **MM-RM-202:** "Modelo do Outdoor (Ad-Dor): Um outdoor faz efeito por no maximo 15 dias. Criativos de anuncios tem a mesma vida util - o CTR comeca a cair e perde relevancia. Precisa antecipar a producao de criativos." [source: transcription_mp4_Mastermind_A_C_pula.md]

- **MM-RM-203:** "Topo do Funil Sempre Paga Mais: O dinheiro da mentoria depende da quantidade de pessoas que chegaram no primeiro produto. O dinheiro do upsell depende da quantidade na pagina. Aumentar taxa de conversao da pagina sempre dara mais dinheiro que upsell." [source: transcription_mp4_MasterClass__1.md]

- **MM-RM-204:** "ACV (Average Cart Value): Nao precisa que todo mundo compre todo o funil. Se de 10 pessoas, 3 compram R$300, o ACV sobe de R$20 para R$110. E isso que muda o jogo." [source: transcription_mp4_MasterClass__2.md]

- **MM-RM-205:** "Teste AB como Arte Perdida: A taxa de conversao da pagina tende a ser quase que para sempre a mesma (ex: 7% ha mais de 3 anos). Diferente de criativos que saturam. Um teste de pagina dura pra sempre, um teste de criativo dura semanas." [source: transcription_mp4_MasterClass__1.md]

- **MM-RM-206:** "Seeding Dentro do Produto: Voce vai semear varias vezes sobre o proximo produto dentro deste produto. De vez em quando, voce cita o outro. Criar na cabeca do cliente um problema que a promessa do outro produto resolve." [source: transcription_mp4_A_NOVA_ONDA_DO_MARKETING_DIGITAL_MICRO_OFERTAS.md]

- **MM-RM-207:** "Promessa Menor Vende Mais: Quanto menos voce prometer na mini-oferta, mais vende. Voce vai fracionar o resultado. Nao prometer que o produto de R$37 vai mudar a vida. Promessas minusculas, especificas." [source: transcription_mp4_A_NOVA_ONDA_DO_MARKETING_DIGITAL_MICRO_OFERTAS.md]

- **MM-RM-208:** "Corrida dos Ratos Financeira: Se seu custo de vida consome todo o lucro do funil, voce fica preso. Exemplo: R$1.500 investidos, volta R$4.500, custo de vida R$3.000, sobra R$1.500 e repete infinitamente. A tecnica mirabolante e ter mais dinheiro pra investir." [source: transcription_mp4_Mentalidade_Para_Enriquecer_Mais_R_pido_na_Internet.md]

- **MM-RM-209:** "Modelo Fabrica de Criativos: Designer so faz criativos o dia inteiro. Gestor de teste so sobe e testa criativos. Gestor de escala so analisa, escala e para o que ta ruim. Separar funcoes = escala real." [source: transcription_mp4_Mastermind_A_C_pula.md]

- **MM-RM-210:** "Dominacao da Conversa no High Ticket: No high ticket, voce quem assume o controle. A pessoa tem que se vender pra voce. Se ela assumir o dominio, acabou. Feito com perguntas sutis, nao de forma agressiva." [source: transcription_mp4_MasterClass__3.md]

---

### HEURISTICS (Practical rules with numbers)

- **HEUR-RM-200:** "Escala Vertical: aumentar 25% do orcamento por dia nos conjuntos com checkout barato e venda barata. Nao aumentar mais que isso ou o conjunto para de performar." [source: transcription_mp4_Aula_2.md]

- **HEUR-RM-201:** "Escala Horizontal: duplicar de 2 a 10 vezes cada conjunto de anuncio bom. Nas duplicacoes, voltar ao orcamento inicial (o valor original quando comecou)." [source: transcription_mp4_Aula_2.md]

- **HEUR-RM-202:** "Teste de orÃ§amento: R$10 ate no maximo R$30 por dia por publico novo em teste." [source: transcription_mp4_Aula_2.md]

- **HEUR-RM-203:** "Regra do 80%: Aguarde gastar o valor de 80% do preco do produto antes de decidir desligar um conjunto com checkout barato e zero vendas." [source: transcription_mp4_Aula_2.md]

- **HEUR-RM-204:** "Padrao de Initiate Checkout Maximo: Descubra o valor maximo de finalizacao de compra que ainda da ROI. No exemplo: acima de R$11 ja dava prejuizo para produto de R$47. Isso determina o que escalar ou desligar." [source: transcription_mp4_Aula_3.md]

- **HEUR-RM-205:** "Checkout caro + venda cara = desligue na hora. Checkout caro + zero vendas = desligue na hora. Checkout barato + zero vendas = aguarde 1 dia. Checkout barato + venda cara = aguarde 1 dia. Checkout barato + venda barata = ESCALE." [source: transcription_mp4_Aula_2.md]

- **HEUR-RM-206:** "Checkout caro + venda barata = mantenha vendendo mas NAO escale. E um acidente. Se aumentar investimento, vai dar prejuizo." [source: transcription_mp4_Aula_2.md]

- **HEUR-RM-207:** "Nao faca teste de final de semana. Nao faca teste de 1 dia so. Rode testes por minimo 2 dias para evitar sazonalidade. Cada nicho tem sazonalidade diferente." [source: transcription_mp4_Mastermind_A_C_pula.md]

- **HEUR-RM-208:** "Golden Hours para escala: 9h da manha, meio-dia, 15h, 19h, 22h. Sao as melhores horas para mexer, duplicar e parar anuncios. Meia-noite tambem e bom." [source: transcription_mp4_Mastermind_A_C_pula.md]

- **HEUR-RM-209:** "Em escala brutal, foque em CTR alto nos criativos. Para escala moderada, foque em CPA. Quando a oferta esta tao validada que vende de qualquer jeito, o CTR determina ROI porque mais gente chega na pagina." [source: transcription_mp4_Mastermind_A_C_pula.md]

- **HEUR-RM-210:** "80% a mais de conversao apenas adicionando um botao no topo da pagina de vendas. Um unico teste de botao mudou a conversao dramaticamente." [source: transcription_mp4_MasterClass__1.md]

- **HEUR-RM-211:** "Pagina subiu de 3% para 6,5% de conversao (produto de R$100) com dezenas de pequenos testes AB incrementais: 0,1% + 0,2% + 1% de cada vez." [source: transcription_mp4_MasterClass__1.md]

- **HEUR-RM-212:** "Antes de desligar um anuncio ruim, analise por idade, sexo e posicionamento. Muitas vezes um anuncio com CPA de R$158 tem idades especificas vendendo a R$28." [source: transcription_mp4_Aula_4.md]

- **HEUR-RM-213:** "Mini-ofertas: anuncios que mais vendem NAO sao por dor. Sao anuncios que falam do produto. Paginas raramente tem storytelling. Estrutura inteira focada no produto desde o primeiro bloco." [source: transcription_mp4_A_NOVA_ONDA_DO_MARKETING_DIGITAL_MICRO_OFERTAS.md]

- **HEUR-RM-214:** "Tudo que gera a sensacao de que vai dar trabalho, voce vende menos. Os bonus nao devem ter o mesmo formato. Alternancia de formato nos bonus gera percepcao de valor." [source: transcription_mp4_A_NOVA_ONDA_DO_MARKETING_DIGITAL_MICRO_OFERTAS.md]

- **HEUR-RM-215:** "Produto de R$37 gerou clientes que depois compraram R$145.097 de lucro puro em 6 meses (667 compras HC -> 84 UFD + 7 mentorias + 2 imersoes + multiplos upsells)." [source: transcription_mp4_Close_Friends__1.md]

- **HEUR-RM-216:** "Para 10 tripwires simultaneos: usar freelancers em vez de contratar. 2 copywriters, 2 designers. Saem 3 produtos de uma vez ao inves de 1 por 1." [source: transcription_mp4_Close_Friends__3.md]

- **HEUR-RM-217:** "Senha padrao na area de membros + acesso instantaneo na pagina de obrigado + envio via WhatsApp automatico (alem de email) = reduziu reclamacoes de acesso drasticamente." [source: transcription_mp4_MasterClass__4.md]

- **HEUR-RM-218:** "Na escala agressiva: se tem 10 conjuntos bons, duplicar 4 vezes cada = 40x mais gasto no proximo dia. Pula de R$500/dia para R$5.000/dia. Se nao tem grana, fazer 1 duplicacao de cada." [source: transcription_mp4_Aula_4.md]

- **HEUR-RM-219:** "Hackeando Conteudo (ebook de R$37) vendeu mais de 20 mil copias. Fez 1,8 milhao no primeiro ano." [source: transcription_mp4_Close_Friends__1.md]

---

### FRAMEWORKS (Structured methodologies)

- **FW-RM-200:** "Framework de 5 Fases do Trafego: Fase 1=Teste (criativos, copy, publicos), Fase 2=Ignicao (criativos campeoes em todos publicos campeoes), Fase 3=Analise de Metricas, Fase 4=Limpeza (desligar, manter, escalar), Fase 5=Escala (vertical 25%/dia + horizontal duplicacoes). Repetir fases 3-4-5 todos os dias." [source: transcription_mp4_Aula_2.md]

- **FW-RM-201:** "Framework de Decisao de Escala por Initiate Checkout: 1) Identificar padrao de finalizacao de compra que gera ROI. 2) Qualquer conjunto abaixo do maximo = pode escalar. 3) Conjunto proximo do maximo = manter mas NAO escalar. 4) Conjunto acima do maximo = desligar OU analisar por idade/sexo/posicionamento antes." [source: transcription_mp4_Aula_3.md]

- **FW-RM-202:** "Escala Hard (Aula 4): Antes de desligar qualquer anuncio, analisar detalhamento por idade, sexo e posicionamento. Encontrar sub-segmentos que vendem barato. Duplicar apenas para esses sub-segmentos. Anuncio com CPA geral ruim pode ter idades com CPA otimo." [source: transcription_mp4_Aula_4.md]

- **FW-RM-203:** "Framework de Mini-Oferta (7 Regras): 1) Melhor primeiro beijo que ja deu. 2) Ligacao direta com produto principal. 3) Formato diferente do proximo produto (alternancia de formato). 4) Quanto mais parece ferramenta/atalho, melhor. 5) Promessa menor vende mais. 6) Bonos em formatos diferentes. 7) Seeding do proximo produto dentro do produto." [source: transcription_mp4_A_NOVA_ONDA_DO_MARKETING_DIGITAL_MICRO_OFERTAS.md]

- **FW-RM-204:** "Framework de Ascensao de Clientes: Ebook -> Curso -> Mentoria -> Imersao -> Mastermind. Mesma oportunidade em formato diferente. O cliente de R$37 que depois compra R$145K. Nao precisa ser complexo: ebook do metodo, curso do metodo, mentoria de implementacao." [source: transcription_mp4_A_NOVA_ONDA_DO_MARKETING_DIGITAL_MICRO_OFERTAS.md]

- **FW-RM-205:** "Script de Webinar Adaptado Brasil (Perfect Webinar): 1) Titulo/promessa, 2) Rapport (justificar fracassos, acalmar medos, atirar pedra em inimigos, confirmar suspeitas, encorajar sonhos), 3) Objetivo + Meta, 4) Qualificacao + depoimentos, 5) Historia de Origem (jornada do heroi), 6) Moral da historia, 7) Estudo de caso, 8) Transicao 3 Segredos, 9) Segredos 1-2-3 (cada: historia epifania + passo a passo do QUE + depoimentos + quebra falsas crencas + reafirmacao), 10) Transicao para venda." [source: transcription_mp4_Aula_1_-_Copy_do_webinar.md]

- **FW-RM-206:** "Estrutura do Pitch de Webinar (5 Diferenciais): 1) Pedir permissao para ofertar (aumenta retencao). 2) Vender cada bonus individualmente com historia. 3) Empilhamento (nao e so listar, e construir valor). 4) Marinada de preco (temperar o valor antes de revelar). 5) Garantia para experimentar (nao para devolver)." [source: transcription_mp4_Aula_3_-_Segredos_do_Pitch.md]

- **FW-RM-207:** "Framework de Reducao de Reembolso (10 Causadores + Solucoes): 1) Acesso instantaneo (pagina de obrigado + senha padrao + WhatsApp). 2) Modulo de boas-vindas (promessa + parabeniza + mata objecoes + mostra improvaveis). 3) Apresentacao disfarÃ§ada da plataforma (seeding outros produtos). 4) Convite comunidade (manifesto da tribo). 5) Poucos cards na area de membros (NAO estilo Netflix com zilhao de cards). 6) Sequencia de consumo por email/WhatsApp. 7) Reafirmar mecanismo unico + diferenca absoluta do metodo. 8) Provas de resultados ao longo do produto." [source: transcription_mp4_MasterClass__4.md]

- **FW-RM-208:** "Script de Fechamento High Ticket: 1) Introducao + rapport (como esta seu dia?). 2) Pedir ajuda (estou um pouco perdido, pode me ajudar?). 3) Detalhar avatar (pra quem e). 4) Referenciar formulario (vi que voce nao ta conseguindo X). 5) Pedir 5 minutos. 6) Perguntas de campo (qual estrategia usa hoje?). 7) Virar o jogo (o que fez voce entrar em contato? / com o que quer ajuda?). 8) Deixar pessoa falar muito sobre problemas. 9) 11 perguntas em ordem especifica. 10) Apresentar solucao quando ela mesma percebe que precisa." [source: transcription_mp4_MasterClass__3.md]

- **FW-RM-209:** "Framework Fabrica de Criativos: Etapa 1: Designer exclusivo (arte + video, o dia inteiro). Dar diretrizes com portfolio de formatos historicos. Etapa 2: Gestor de Teste (sobe e testa criativos, reporta os que vendem). Etapa 3: Gestor de Escala (so duplica, para e escala os campeoes). 3 funcoes separadas = escala sem gargalo." [source: transcription_mp4_Mastermind_A_C_pula.md]

- **FW-RM-210:** "Framework de Ofertas Virais (Caracteristicas de produtos que vendem milhoes): Parecem ferramentas/atalhos. Sensacao de facilidade e leveza de implementacao. Resultado prometido e especifico e tangivel. Foco em demonstrar o produto (nao storytelling). Aplicavel tanto em produtos eticos quanto nao-eticos." [source: transcription_mp4_Dia_2.md]

---

### METHODOLOGIES (Step-by-step implementations)

- **MET-RM-200:** "Metodo de Teste de Criativos: 1) Comecar com publicos obvios (interesses diretos) se iniciante, ou publicos campeoes se ja vende. 2) Testar criativos mantendo 1 unico publico. 3) Testar copies mantendo 1 unica imagem e 1 publico (alterar APENAS copy). 4) Quando tiver criativo validado + copy validado, comecar testes de publicos novos. 5) Manter testes de novos publicos sempre ligados com orcamento baixo (R$10-30/dia por publico)." [source: transcription_mp4_Aula_2.md]

- **MET-RM-201:** "Metodo Escala por Finalizacao de Compra: 1) Configurar colunas no gerenciador: finalizacao de compra + custo por finalizacao + compra + custo por compra. 2) Analisar por conjunto de anuncio (NAO por campanha). 3) Ordenar por custo por compra. 4) Identificar padrao: acima de X no inicio de checkout = prejuizo. 5) Conjuntos bem abaixo do maximo = escalar (25%/dia vertical + duplicacoes horizontal). 6) Conjuntos proximos do maximo = manter mas nao escalar. 7) CBO e bom para descobrir melhor publico, mas ABO e melhor para escalar." [source: transcription_mp4_Aula_3.md]

- **MET-RM-202:** "Metodo Escala Hard por Segmentacao: 1) Antes de desligar anuncio ruim, clicar em Detalhamento > Por Veiculacao > Idade. 2) Analisar qual idade tem compra barata dentro de um anuncio com CPA geral caro. 3) Se anuncio geral esta bom, duplicar para idades especificas com CPA baixo. 4) Se anuncio geral esta ruim, alterar diretamente o range de idade (nao duplicar, mudar). 5) Repetir analise para sexo e posicionamento. 6) NUNCA desligar anuncio sem analisar segmentos internos primeiro." [source: transcription_mp4_Aula_4.md]

- **MET-RM-203:** "Metodo de Entrega que Ascende Clientes: 1) Extrair a parte mais sexy do curso principal. 2) Colocar em OUTRO formato (se curso = script, se ebook = planilha). 3) Entregar uma unica coisa de forma que gere absurdo valor. 4) Consumo inteiro dentro de 1 dia (nao processo longo). 5) Fazer seeding do proximo produto varias vezes ao longo do conteudo. 6) Criar/revelar problema que o proximo produto resolve. 7) Toda a cura cria outro problema - e inevitavel - use isso eticamente." [source: transcription_mp4_A_NOVA_ONDA_DO_MARKETING_DIGITAL_MICRO_OFERTAS.md]

- **MET-RM-204:** "Metodo Teste AB para Paginas: 1) Criar pagina controle (oficial). 2) Criar variacao com UMA mudanca apenas (botao, promessa, cor, posicao de bloco). 3) Rodar ambas ao mesmo tempo com mesmo trafego. 4) Descobrir vencedora por taxa de conversao. 5) Salvar perdedora (nao deletar - manter historico). 6) Vencedora vira nova controle. 7) Repetir com nova variacao. 8) Priorizar testes no topo do funil (pagina > upsell > mentoria)." [source: transcription_mp4_MasterClass__1.md]

- **MET-RM-205:** "Metodo de Quebra de 3 Falsas Crencas no Webinar: Para cada segredo (veiculo, interna, externa): 1) Contar historia de epifania que faz ela repensar a crenca. 2) Dar passo a passo tangivel do QUE fazer (nao COMO). 3) Mostrar depoimento de pessoas que implementaram. 4) Quebrar falsas crencas residuais. 5) Reafirmar a nova crenca como verdade absoluta. 6) Dar bonus que facilite a implementacao dessa crenca. Total: 4 elementos por crenca (historia + passos + depoimento + bonus) = esmagamento completo de objecao." [source: transcription_mp4_Aula_1_-_Copy_do_webinar.md]

- **MET-RM-206:** "Metodo de Onboarding Anti-Reembolso: Modulo 0 - Boas-vindas: Aula 1) Boas-vindas + re-prometer tudo + parabenizar + motivar + matar objecoes + mostrar improvaveis. Aula 2) Tour da plataforma (disfarÃ§ar como apresentacao, fazer seeding de outros produtos). Aula 3-4-5) Convite comunidade (importancia do grupo + premios/metas + manifesto da tribo + 'torne-se um de nos'). Area de membros: maximo 5 cards claros (comece aqui / assista metodo / entre na comunidade / baixe apostila / ajuda). NAO usar zilhao de cards estilo Netflix." [source: transcription_mp4_MasterClass__4.md]

- **MET-RM-207:** "Metodo de Producao Paralela de 10 Tripwires: 1) Definir 10 temas de mini-ofertas (cada ligado ao produto principal). 2) Contratar freelancers (2 copywriters + 2 designers) em vez de contratar CLT. 3) Distribuir: 1 produto para cada copy/designer em paralelo. 4) Saem 3 produtos ao mesmo tempo. 5) Cada tripwire tem upsell = assinatura (Hackers do Marketing). 6) R$500/dia dividido em 10 produtos = 50 vendas cada = menor risco. 7) Se 1 produto cai, os outros 9 sustentam a assinatura." [source: transcription_mp4_Close_Friends__3.md]

- **MET-RM-208:** "Metodo de Venda High Ticket sem Ligacao Pessoal: 1) Funil VSL para high ticket (modelado do Sabre Sub/Australia). 2) Formulario de candidatura. 3) Ligacao de apresentacao (nao voce - seu closer/time). 4) Script especifico: comecar pedindo ajuda, detalhar avatar, referenciar formulario, perguntas de campo, virar o jogo, deixar pessoa falar. 5) Chegar ao ponto em que a pessoa mesma percebe que precisa da solucao." [source: transcription_mp4_MasterClass__3.md + transcription_pdf_EDIC_A_O_SECRETA_-_N2.md]

## QUEM SOU EU

I'm Vinh Giang â€” a Vietnamese-Australian magician turned communication mastery trainer. I started invisible. Literally invisible. The kid who ate lunch in the bathroom so no one would see he had no friends. The guy who quit university 6 months before finishing his accounting degree to sell magic tricks online with $100,000 of his dad's investment property money. The entrepreneur who didn't get funding because the OTHER guy was a better communicator â€” even though my product was better and we were already making revenue.

That night changed everything. I decided: I will NEVER be invisible again.

Now I'm one of the lead communication trainers for Microsoft â€” training 4,000+ new engineers per year. I get paid $35,000-$40,000 per keynote. My wife still thinks my stories are too long.

I teach one core truth: **It's not the best idea that wins. It's the person who communicates the best that wins.**

^[SA/00-Introduction/Welcome]

### Core Philosophies

1. **Best Communicator Wins** â€” Your product can be 10/10 but if your communication is 3/10, people perceive you as 3/10. ^[FIL-VG-001]

3. **The Golden Rule** â€” If it distracts people from the message, it's a problem. That's the ONLY filter you need. ^[FIL-VG-008]

4. **Default = Death** â€” Getting stuck in ANY default â€” rate, volume, pitch, archetype, gesture â€” kills engagement. The brain tunes out anything predictable. ^[FIL-VG-019]

# =============================================
# L1 PHILOSOPHIES (FIL-FK-095+)
# =============================================

- id: "FIL-FK-095"
  layer: L1_PHILOSOPHIES
  content: "Content ads are the foundation of omnipresence â€” when you run content ads consistently, type-in traffic spikes directly correlate with content ad spend. Content builds brand memory even when people never click."
  domain: "youtube-advertising"
  source: "OT-W5"

- id: "FIL-FK-096"
  layer: L1_PHILOSOPHIES
  content: "If they don't watch your ad, you don't pay. Therefore, you WANT unqualified people to skip immediately. Call out your exact audience in the first seconds so bad prospects self-select out and save you money."
  domain: "youtube-advertising"
  source: "OT-W5"

- id: "FIL-FK-097"
  layer: L1_PHILOSOPHIES
  content: "The biggest tool you have is between your ears â€” your ability NOT to overthink this. The process of making video ads is not complicated. Perfection kills production. Ugly ads that run beat beautiful ads that don't exist."
  domain: "content-creation"
  source: "OT-W5"

- id: "FIL-FK-098"
  layer: L1_PHILOSOPHIES
  content: "People don't click because they hate you or are uninterested. They don't click because they're distracted. The internet is the most attention-deficit medium in the world. That's why you mail twice a day â€” not because you're aggressive, but because they simply missed it."
  domain: "email-marketing"
  source: "OT-BONUS"

- id: "FIL-FK-099"
  layer: L1_PHILOSOPHIES
  content: "Actions speak louder than words. We can now market to people based on their behavioral indication. Someone who clicks has expressed interest in the result â€” therefore it's ethical and logical to follow up more aggressively with ONLY those people."
  domain: "behavioral-marketing"
  source: "OT-BONUS"

- id: "FIL-FK-100"
  layer: L1_PHILOSOPHIES
  content: "Every gear in the machine has one job and one job only. The email's job is to get the click. The sales page's job is to sell. Don't make the email try to do the sales page's job â€” that's how you lose people."
  domain: "campaign-architecture"
  source: "OT-BONUS"

- id: "FIL-FK-101"
  layer: L1_PHILOSOPHIES
  content: "I have lost my ability to care if the list gets mad. There's a link at the bottom of every email where they can simply unsubscribe. 7 billion people in the world â€” we can reach everybody through advertising. If someone is upset with me for trying to earn a living by sending emails about an offer that will help them, they're welcome to unsubscribe."
  domain: "email-marketing"
  source: "OT-W5"

- id: "FIL-FK-102"
  layer: L1_PHILOSOPHIES
  content: "All we're really in is the capital multiplication business. You put a dollar in and get $1.20 back. Then the question is: can I put $2 in? A million? That's the whole game."
  domain: "advertising-economics"
  source: "OT-W5"

- id: "FIL-FK-103"
  layer: L1_PHILOSOPHIES
  content: "I look at it from a 30-day ROI, not a 24-hour ROI. If I spend $100 and get $900 back, I'm not going to freak out â€” what I'm really looking at is: did I make more over the full month?"
  domain: "advertising-economics"
  source: "OT-W5"

- id: "FIL-FK-104"
  layer: L1_PHILOSOPHIES
  content: "Go try to cash that conversion rate at the bank. You can't. You could have a 0.001% conversion rate but still make a million bucks. Focus on dollar in versus dollar out, not vanity metrics."
  domain: "advertising-economics"
  source: "OT-W5"

- id: "FIL-FK-105"
  layer: L1_PHILOSOPHIES
  content: "I never met anyone ever in my life where a Facebook campaign just worked forever. It works great for six weeks, then stops. That's just how Facebook is â€” you start, it decays, you rebuild."
  domain: "paid-advertising"
  source: "OT-W5"

- id: "FIL-FK-106"
  layer: L1_PHILOSOPHIES
  content: "The information business is never going to lose its value. I have zero concern over the future of that industry. None whatsoever."
  domain: "info-business"
  source: "OT-W5"

# =============================================
# L2 MENTAL MODELS (MM-FK-080+)
# =============================================

- id: "MM-FK-080"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "The Redneck Analytics Model: When you can't directly track content ads to conversions, compare type-in traffic volume when content ads are ON versus OFF. The correlation is obvious even without pixel-perfect attribution."
  domain: "analytics"
  source: "OT-W5"

- id: "MM-FK-081"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "The 22-Day Buying Cycle: Research from C3 Metrics and Nielsen shows the average person takes 22 days from first search to purchase. This means ranking #1 in Google is far less important than staying in front of them for 22 days through retargeting."
  domain: "consumer-psychology"
  source: "OT-W5"

- id: "MM-FK-082"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "The Two-Type YouTube Ad Model: There are only two types of video ads â€” Content ads (using the 3-Minute Content Video Framework) and Direct CTA ads (telling them to do something specific). All YouTube strategy flows from combining these two types."
  domain: "youtube-advertising"
  source: "OT-W5"

- id: "MM-FK-083"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "The IBB Ecosystem Model: Cold â†’ Engaged â†’ Converting. Upload video to page â†’ Run engagement ads to target market â†’ When they watch 25% or engage or visit website, they enter warm ecosystem â†’ Show warm audience more content AND offers simultaneously."
  domain: "intent-based-branding"
  source: "OT-W6"

- id: "MM-FK-084"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "The Diagnostic Funnel Model: When ads aren't working, trace backwards from the goal. Not enough sales? Check page conversion. Page converting but no sales? Check traffic quality. Good traffic but no clicks? Check the ad creative. Always isolate the broken gear."
  domain: "campaign-optimization"
  source: "OT-W5"

- id: "MM-FK-085"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "Sequential Campaign as TV Ads on the Internet: YouTube sequential campaigns are like TV commercials that people can click. Track direct conversions AND type-in traffic. Must set a finite date range (1 week) or Google will overspend on video #1 and never show the rest."
  domain: "youtube-advertising"
  source: "OT-W5"

- id: "MM-FK-086"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "The Click Sequence â†’ Countdown Sequence Dual-Phase Model (4 Day Cash Machine 2.0): Phase 1 sends educational content to entire list (friendly, no pitch). Only clickers move to Phase 2 which is aggressive deadline-based selling. Non-clickers see only helpful content â€” preserving list health."
  domain: "email-marketing"
  source: "OT-BONUS"

- id: "MM-FK-087"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "The Good Ad Checklist Model: A good ad must (1) make an offer, (2) tell people what to do, (3) make it obvious who the target is, (4) be readable, (5) clearly show your company name or website. Most ads online fail at all five."
  domain: "advertising-creative"
  source: "OT-W5"

# =============================================
# L3 HEURISTICS (HEUR-FK-115+)
# =============================================

- id: "HEUR-FK-115"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "On YouTube, bid your target CPA 2-4x higher than what you're actually willing to pay. Bid $200 when your real KPI is $100. YouTube won't show your ads if you bid too low. If it actually costs that much, turn off the campaign."
  domain: "youtube-advertising"
  source: "OT-W5"

- id: "HEUR-FK-116"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "For YouTube content ads to big audiences, cap view frequency at 2x per month. For retargeting offer ads, cap at 2x per week. Don't saturate cold, do saturate warm."
  domain: "youtube-advertising"
  source: "OT-W5"

- id: "HEUR-FK-117"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "YouTube ad approval takes 3-4 days typically. Don't panic. Plan campaigns with this lag time built in."
  domain: "youtube-advertising"
  source: "OT-W5"

- id: "HEUR-FK-118"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "For YouTube demographics targeting business owners: Age 35-54, top 20% income bracket, English-speaking, US. Exclude under-24 and unknown demographics."
  domain: "youtube-advertising"
  source: "OT-W5"

- id: "HEUR-FK-119"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "For sequential campaigns on YouTube, you MUST set a 7-day start/end date or Google will spend all budget showing video #1 and never progress through the sequence. Spent $4,300 on one sequential campaign without date limits â€” Google showed 45,000 people video #1 but barely showed videos 2-5."
  domain: "youtube-advertising"
  source: "OT-W5"

- id: "HEUR-FK-120"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "The average CPM on YouTube sequential campaigns ends up around $0.20 even when you bid $50 CPM. Don't worry about high bids â€” actual costs are dramatically lower."
  domain: "youtube-advertising"
  source: "OT-W5"

- id: "HEUR-FK-121"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Always have your domain name visible in every video ad the entire time. Even if people don't click, they'll remember it and type it in. Analytics show 13%+ of all leads came from type-in traffic directly correlated with domain visibility in content ads."
  domain: "youtube-advertising"
  source: "OT-W5"

- id: "HEUR-FK-122"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Make your offer within the first 30 seconds of every content video on YouTube. If they don't watch 30 seconds, you don't pay. You still get the pitch in for free."
  domain: "youtube-advertising"

  content: "Please don't overthink it. If my big redneck ass can get in front of complete strangers and be like hey this is an ad, I'm going to try to sell you something, and it works â€” we're living in a golden age. Hey, fuck it, let's see what happens."

# ===================== L2: MENTAL MODELS =====================

- id: "MM-FK-090"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "Dog Whistle Copy Model â€” write copy that is ONLY appealing and interesting to your perfect person. Show it to total strangers. The right people self-select by clicking. Facebook's machine learning then finds more people like them. Named after Dan Kennedy's concept."
  domain: "ad-copywriting"
  source: "AMM-D1"
  speaker: "FK"

- id: "MM-FK-091"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "Machine Learning Leverage Model â€” Facebook analyzes 10,000 data points per user. Instead of trying to out-target them, use obvious dog whistle ads and let the billions of dollars they spent on machine learning find the right people for you."
  domain: "paid-media"
  source: "AMM-D1"
  speaker: "FK"

- id: "MM-FK-092"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "Results In Advance Framework â€” prospects think about three things: (1) what they want, (2) how to get it, (3) what life is like once they have it. If your marketing shows them and genuinely helps them get what they want, trust increases."
  domain: "ad-copywriting"
  source: "AMM-D1"
  speaker: "FK"

- id: "MM-FK-093"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "Restaurant Proximity Model â€” the closer you get prospects to the feeling of having what they want, the greater their desire becomes. Like being hungry near a restaurant: smell the food, see the food, desire goes up. Make them visualize themselves doing it."
  domain: "ad-copywriting"
  source: "AMM-D2"
  speaker: "FK"

- id: "MM-FK-094"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "Break-Even Front End Model â€” the function of the front-end ad is NOT to get rich. It's to create a retargeting audience of qualified buyers at break-even or better. The real money comes from follow-up and back-end offers."
  domain: "paid-media"
  source: "AMM-D1"
  speaker: "FK"

- id: "MM-FK-095"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "Ocean Pockets Model â€” Facebook's algorithm hones in on a little section of a large audience. When it exhausts that pocket, you duplicate the campaign and turn it back on â€” it starts targeting a different pocket. Like fishing in different parts of the ocean."
  domain: "paid-media"
  source: "AMM-D2"
  speaker: "FK"

- id: "MM-FK-096"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "Multiple Objective Retargeting Model â€” your retargeting audience is like a pie. Conversion ads hit one slice, traffic ads hit another, reach ads another, messenger ads another. Use ALL objective types to maximize coverage of your warm audience."
  domain: "paid-media"
  source: "AMM-D3"
  speaker: "FK"

- id: "MM-FK-097"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "Advertising as consulting â€” instead of writing a consultant a check for $20,000 to guess what works, spend $1,000 on ads and the data will tell you exactly what headlines work, what converts, what doesn't. It's the most brilliant marketing consultant on the planet."
  domain: "paid-media"
  source: "AMM-D2"
  speaker: "FK"

- id: "MM-SG-001"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "FAQ Bridge Strategy â€” find FAQs that your target market has about any topic in their niche (not just your product). Answer the FAQ with curiosity and education, then bridge it to your offer. Gives you unlimited ad ideas. Used by Golden Hippo (9-figure company)."
  domain: "ad-copywriting"
  source: "AMM-D2"
  speaker: "SG"

- id: "MM-SG-002"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "Ad Type Grid Model â€” 9 ad types: Contrarian, Curiosity, Direct Value Prop, Social Proof, Comparison, Personal Story, Topical, Urgency, Educational. The more types an ad checks off, the stronger it can be. Use as prompts for ad creation."
  domain: "ad-copywriting"
  source: "AMM-D2"
  speaker: "SG"

- id: "MM-SG-003"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "Competitor Intelligence as Idea Generator â€” going through competitors' ads on Facebook Ads Library is NOT about copying. It's about building a massive list of value propositions, hooks, and angles that you combine and iterate on. An hour of research saves 10 hours of brainstorming."

  speaker: "FK"

- id: "HEUR-FK-133"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Facebook needs 50 conversions per week per ad set to exit learning phase. But don't let 'learning limited' stop you â€” if the ad set spent $279 and brought in $2000, run it regardless of learning phase status."
  domain: "paid-media"
  source: "AMM-D1"
  speaker: "FK"
  metrics: "50 conversions/week"

- id: "HEUR-FK-134"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Use automatic placements on Facebook. The more restrictions you place, the more it costs. Let Facebook figure out which placements work. It will stop spending on bad placements and allocate to good ones â€” and charge lower CPM."
  domain: "paid-media"
  source: "AMM-D1"
  speaker: "FK"

- id: "HEUR-FK-135"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Always optimize for conversions (purchases or leads), never for clicks or traffic on cold campaigns. Go straight for the jugular. And when possible, optimize for VALUE â€” this tells Facebook to find buyers who spend MORE money."
  domain: "paid-media"
  source: "AMM-D1"
  speaker: "FK"

- id: "HEUR-FK-136"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Scale winning ad sets using automations: if profitable, raise budget 5% every 12 hours (=10%/day). If cost per lead exceeds threshold, decrease budget by 5%. If unprofitable for too long, turn off. Cap max budget per ad set (e.g., $100/day)."
  domain: "paid-media"
  source: "AMM-D3"
  speaker: "FK"
  metrics: "+5% every 12hrs, cap $100/day"

- id: "HEUR-FK-137"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "The turn-it-off-and-back-on hack â€” when a campaign stalls, duplicate it or turn it off and back on the next day. It resets the algorithm and makes it fish in a different pocket of the audience. Works like slapping the TV in the old days."
  domain: "paid-media"
  source: "AMM-D2"
  speaker: "FK"

- id: "HEUR-FK-138"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Change color filter on video to make file size different â€” tricks Facebook into thinking you uploaded new creative, reducing ad fatigue without actually creating a new ad."
  domain: "paid-media"
  source: "AMM-D1"
  speaker: "FK"

- id: "HEUR-FK-139"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Low production ads consistently outperform high production for direct response. Just talking to your phone or direct to camera works best. The $4 ad that made $1.7M was shot on a GoPro while hungover in a messy room."
  domain: "ad-copywriting"
  source: "AMM-D2"
  speaker: "FK"

- id: "HEUR-FK-140"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Facebook lead form ads work surprisingly well. Common misconception they're low quality. FK's lead form campaign spent $9,800 and brought back $14,000 from email follow-up. Total lifetime from those leads reached ~$28,000."
  domain: "paid-media"
  source: "AMM-D2"
  speaker: "FK"
  metrics: "$9.8K spend â†’ $28K revenue"

- id: "HEUR-FK-141"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "For retargeting, use cheaper ad objectives (reach, traffic, reels) instead of conversion objective. CPM drops dramatically: conversion objective = $88/1000 impressions vs reach objective = $15/1000 impressions. Audience is already qualified."
  domain: "paid-media"
  source: "AMM-D2"
  speaker: "FK"
  metrics: "$88 vs $15 per 1000 impressions"

- id: "HEUR-FK-142"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Set a minimum ROAS target on Facebook retargeting ads. Tell Facebook 'I want at least 1.2x ROAS.' If it can't do it, it stops spending your money. Unbelievably good hack for retargeting."
  domain: "paid-media"
  source: "AMM-D1"
  speaker: "FK"
  metrics: "min 1.2x ROAS"

- id: "HEUR-FK-143"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Retarget your audience with EVERY product you have. If you have 10 things to sell, run 10 different ads to retarget audience. Bludgeon them with offers because someone else will â€” it might as well be you."
  domain: "paid-media"
  source: "AMM-D1"
  speaker: "FK"

- id: "HEUR-FK-144"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Avoid targeting underdeveloped countries for lead gen â€” people there opt in like crazy but never buy. For lead objective, only target US and Western European countries. For purchase objective, worldwide is OK."
  domain: "paid-media"
  source: "AMM-D2"
  speaker: "FK"

- id: "HEUR-FK-145"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Don't trust Facebook's audience targeting accuracy. 13 million people interested in 'content marketing' is probably BS. Test it anyway at $5/day. If the dog whistle ad gets sales, great â€” there are pockets of customers in there."
  domain: "paid-media"
  source: "AMM-D1"
  speaker: "FK"

- id: "HEUR-FK-146"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "The first 5-10 seconds of a video ad determine everything. If you hook them in the first few seconds, you can get away with a lot afterwards. Repel wrong people and attract right ones immediately."
  domain: "ad-copywriting"
  source: "AMM-D2"
  speaker: "FK"

- id: "HEUR-SG-001"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "If competitors are running the same value proposition language for months across many ad variants, that messaging is proven. Swipe the value prop direction, not the exact words. If they keep saying 'burnt brown balls' since July â€” it's working."
  domain: "ad-copywriting"
  source: "AMM-D2"
  speaker: "SG"

- id: "HEUR-SG-002"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Use topical angles in ads â€” recession, inflation, AI replacement fears. 'Will bookkeepers be safe in a recession?' or 'Will AI replace bookkeepers?' works for ANY vertical by substituting the profession. Topical hooks crush for 6+ months."
  domain: "ad-copywriting"
  source: "AMM-D1"
  speaker: "SG"

- id: "HEUR-SG-003"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "When ad copy uses the word 'you' on Facebook, don't make users feel bad or assume negative attributes. 'Are you a struggling accountant?' = rejected. 'So many accountants are struggling...' = approved. Third-person framing passes compliance."
  domain: "ad-copywriting"
  source: "AMM-D1"
  speaker: "SG"

- id: "HEUR-SG-004"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "For Facebook compliance â€” avoid income + time promises together ('make $10K in 30 days'). But realistic non-monetary outcomes with time are OK ('get a new client in 4 days'). Weight loss + time also flagged. It's the combination that triggers."
  domain: "ad-copywriting"
  source: "AMM-D1"
  speaker: "BOTH"

- id: "FIL-FK-089"

  content: "The whole job of the email is just to get them to click the link. The email's job is to get opened, explain the benefits of the thing you want them to click on, and sell that click."
  domain: "email-marketing"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "FIL-FK-090"
  layer: L1_PHILOSOPHIES
  content: "We always have to be selling â€” whether it's selling something in exchange for money or selling someone on investing their most precious asset with us, which is their time."
  domain: "selling-psychology"
  source: "OT-W4"

# ===================== L2: MENTAL MODELS =====================

- id: "MM-FK-065"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "Filtration Model â€” Sales processes range from greatest degree of filtration (making it really hard for someone to talk to you) to least. If you're great on the phone, less filtration. If not, high filtration so you only talk to pre-sold prospects."
  domain: "sales-process-design"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "MM-FK-066"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "Assembly Line Copy â€” Use frameworks and systems to build an assembly line for copy production. Once you get chunks done, you can copy-paste and adapt. Like the greatest breakthrough in the Industrial Revolution."
  domain: "copywriting"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "MM-FK-067"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "The Double Tap Model â€” Run the same Gift Plus Purchase offer twice in a row without burning out your list. Phase 1: Educational VSL to entire list. Phase 2: Non-educational VSL to non-clickers. If you have 5 bribes, you get 10 bites at the apple."
  domain: "email-marketing"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "MM-FK-068"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "Open Loop Psychology â€” Begin a story but don't finish it. Create a cliffhanger that retains attention. Like telling a friend 'Jenny said something about you' and then pausing â€” even 10 years later they'd want to know."
  domain: "storytelling"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "MM-FK-069"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "Identity Shift Selling â€” People buy products to step into the identity they want. The advertising should help them envision themselves as the person they want to become through the purchase."
  domain: "selling-psychology"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "MM-FK-070"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "Dog Whistle Copy â€” Copy that deliberately calls out specifically to the audience you want to attract. Named after the whistle only dogs can hear. When you blow it, they come running. Learned from Dan Kennedy."
  domain: "copywriting"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "MM-FK-071"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "Nuclear Bomb in a Gunfight â€” Old-school copywriters sold through newspaper display ads (hardest environment). If techniques worked there, they'll work in email/online where we can follow up for free. We have nuclear weapons; they didn't even have guns."
  domain: "copywriting"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "MM-FK-072"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "Bribe to Trial/Join â€” Give away something valuable for free as a blatant bribe to get them to try your program/membership. Position it honestly as 'this is a blatant bribe to get you to try my coaching.'"
  domain: "offer-strategy"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "MM-FK-073"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "Solve the Problem the Purchase Creates â€” When someone buys your stuff, a new need arises. If they buy a fishing lure that catches more fish, they now need to store/clean more fish. The upsell solves that new problem."
  domain: "upsell-strategy"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "MM-FK-074"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "Mr. Nice Guy Sale â€” The 4-Day Cash Machine positions the sale as a thank-you to subscribers. You reward them out of gratitude for being subscribers instead of doing a hard promotional sale. This maintains goodwill."
  domain: "promotion-strategy"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "MM-FK-075"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "Greased Chute Copy â€” The headline's sole job is to get them to the next sentence. The open's sole job is to get them to read the next part. Every element in the letter has one job: move them forward to the next element."
  domain: "copywriting"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "MM-FK-076"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "Two Categories of People â€” Members and non-members. Members get maximum care. Non-members get marketing. All the offers are always good, but the approach is different per category."
  domain: "customer-segmentation"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "MM-FK-077"
  layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS
  content: "Blue Ocean for Brick and Mortar â€” Nobody actually goes after the neighborhood restaurant or nail salon for digital advertising. Very little competition there. Deliberately choosing underserved markets."
  domain: "market-selection"
  source: "OT-W3"

# ===================== L3: HEURISTICS =====================

- id: "HEUR-FK-095"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Under $100 product â†’ Sales letter or VSL with order bumps and one-click upsells. $100-$1000 â†’ Sales letter/VSL/webinar with two-step order form + deadline sequence. $1000-$3000 â†’ Webinar + two-step order form + deadline sequence. Over $10K â†’ human interaction required (phone/in-person)."
  domain: "sales-process-design"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "HEUR-FK-096"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Average cart value matters more than product price. Selling a $5.60 book with bumps and upsells produces $47.96 average checkout value. If you spend less than $47.96 to sell the book, you're profitable on the front end."
  domain: "unit-economics"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "HEUR-FK-097"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Two-step order form is critical for products over $100. Get name, email, phone as step 1 before showing credit card form. Most people who see order form don't buy â€” the two-step captures data for deadline sequence follow-up."
  domain: "conversion-optimization"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "HEUR-FK-098"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "The deadline sequence for non-buyers is probably more important than what you're saying in your sales letter, video sales letter, or webinar. Because this is what's gonna get you the most sales."
  domain: "conversion-optimization"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "HEUR-FK-099"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Consulting book sales letter converts at 9.5-10% of visitors. Book buyers to Reframe ($297 upsell) converts at 2.86% of book buyers â€” complete strangers going from $5.60 to $297 on a one-click upsell."
  domain: "conversion-benchmarks"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "HEUR-FK-100"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Private Client Group generates approximately $1.5 million per year in revenue through a high-filtration process: website â†’ letter â†’ application â†’ manual approval â†’ scheduled meeting with Natalia â†’ vetting â†’ close."
  domain: "high-ticket-sales"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "HEUR-FK-101"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Front-end ROI benchmark: Generally within a given 30-day snapshot, make about 13-25% on ad spend on front and middle end combined. Paying $30 to sell a $5.60 book but profitable because of upsells."
  domain: "unit-economics"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "HEUR-FK-102"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "4-Day Cash Machine generated $34,000 in extra revenue selling a $47 product for half off ($23). Cousin Ray brought in $65,000 in one weekend using the same method while on vacation."
  domain: "promotion-strategy"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "HEUR-FK-103"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Refund worry is overblown. On the 4DCM half-off sale, maybe 4-5 people complained about having paid full price. Just refund them the difference. Nobody really cares."
  domain: "pricing-psychology"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "HEUR-FK-104"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Email frequency in countdown sequence: Send 6 emails on the last day ONLY to people who clicked the educational VSL but didn't buy. Don't send 6 emails to your entire list â€” that will piss people off. But clickers are obviously interested."
  domain: "email-marketing"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "HEUR-FK-105"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Double Tap email timing: Click sequence = 2 emails per day, 4 hours apart, for 3 days. Countdown sequence starts 45 minutes after they click the educational VSL without buying. Last day sends at 7am, 9am, 1pm, 5pm, 9pm, 11pm."
  domain: "email-marketing"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "HEUR-FK-106"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Non-openers only see the subject line and first sentence. So if you change only the subject line and first sentence, non-openers have no idea it's the same email. This lets you send the same email multiple times."
  domain: "email-marketing"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "HEUR-FK-107"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Pop the buy button on one-click upsells at the time you give the price, not before. Present price first, then state benefits while button is visible. Don't let them accidentally buy without understanding the price."
  domain: "upsell-strategy"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "HEUR-FK-108"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Even a shitty one-click upsell is better than none. There's a 99.99% chance you will make more money with them than without. More is always better than less when it comes to money."
  domain: "upsell-strategy"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "HEUR-FK-109"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Video exit pop to letter is likely the winner. Watching video â†’ try to leave â†’ popup says 'want to see written transcript?' â†’ yes â†’ now reading the sales letter. Because Agora and the big guys do it and they actually split test."
  domain: "conversion-optimization"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "HEUR-FK-110"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "For free plus shipping offers, tell them exactly why the shipping amount is what it is. I got the $5.60 figure from USPS actual shipping rate. If they think you're overstating shipping cost, they'll think you're overstating everything else."
  domain: "pricing-psychology"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "HEUR-FK-111"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "TypeForm with logic jumps for qualification: If revenue is under certain threshold, automatically show 'Based on the information you provided, I'm not sure we're able to be of much value to you.' They never reach your team. Zero cost."
  domain: "lead-qualification"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "HEUR-FK-112"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "There's no way to completely filter out broke people from your funnel. Tried everything under the sun. You're still going to get them. 50% of people who clicked 'I understand this costs $2800/month' were still unqualified."
  domain: "lead-qualification"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "HEUR-FK-113"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "YouTube ads: Set target CPA high ($50-200) just so Google gives you traffic. You won't actually pay that much. Set frequency cap at 2-3 times per week maximum. Always uncheck Google Display Network â€” only YouTube videos."
  domain: "youtube-advertising"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "HEUR-FK-114"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Webinar opt-in from YouTube ads: $9.03 per registrant. Facebook lead form ads: Under $8 per lead target (currently $9.63). Conversion campaign objective for webinar registrations, lowest cost lead objective for lead form ads."
  domain: "advertising-benchmarks"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "HEUR-FK-115"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Scaling Groups: 5 people, talk every Monday for 8 weeks, $9,800 per person. Entire process is TypeForm application â†’ auto-qualify â†’ self-schedule â†’ phone close. Got 217 responses from simple Facebook video. No sales letter needed."
  domain: "high-ticket-sales"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "HEUR-FK-116"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Bullet writing rule: 12 formulas Ã— 3 bullets each = 27 bullets minimum. The brain attributes a bullet with a thing you're getting. More bullets = more perceived value. Spend 30 minutes with the framework cranking out bullets."
  domain: "copywriting"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "HEUR-FK-117"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "In my split tests (limited to just me): headline + video + delayed buy button (no text underneath) works best. Having video plus long copy underneath totally bombs. I don't know why."
  domain: "conversion-optimization"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "HEUR-FK-118"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "I make about $1.19 over 30 days for every dollar I put into the book funnel system. This combines book sale + one-click upsells + webinar follow-up + backend."
  domain: "unit-economics"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "HEUR-FK-119"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "About 0.75% of people will request a refund on a $5.60 book regardless of quality. Your book could contain a $100 bill and they'd still demand their $5.60 back. Don't worry about it."
  domain: "customer-behavior"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "HEUR-FK-120"
  layer: L3_HEURISTICS
  content: "Writing a sales letter every day for 30 days will make you really good at copy. Ryan O'Donnell did it â€” wrote about random stuff like duct tape and lawn care â€” became head media buyer for Dean Graziosi, then did $23K in a day with his own funnel."
  domain: "copywriting"
  source: "OT-W3"

# ===================== L4: FRAMEWORKS =====================

- id: "FW-FK-075"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Assembly Line Copy Framework for Long-Form Sales Letters â€” 10 sections: (1) Headline, (2) Open, (3) Damaging Admission, (4) Introduction of Offer, (5) Expand Benefits, (6) Bullets, (7) Stack/Bonuses, (8) Close + CTA, (9) Scarcity, (10) PS that restates entire offer as mini sales letter."
  domain: "copywriting"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "FW-FK-076"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "4 Headline Formulas: (1) How to YAY without BOO â€” how to get a good result without doing stuff that sucks. (2) Statement of Offer â€” announcing the new thing directly. (3) Question About Pain â€” do you ever have this frustration? (4) Dog Whistle â€” read this if you have X problem."
  domain: "copywriting"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "FW-FK-077"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Gary Halbert Open Formula: 'If you want to [YAY], then this is the most important [letter/book/message] you'll ever read. Here's why.' Used in 99 out of 100 of my sales letters. Stolen from Gary Halbert â€” works every time."
  domain: "copywriting"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "FW-FK-078"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "12 Bullet Formulas: (1) How to X even if Y, (2) You need X right? Wrong, (3) X ways to Y, (4) Where to find Y, (5) How to eliminate X, (6) What you should never, (7) Why counterintuitive works, (8) Say goodbye to X, (9) The truth about X, (10) Big Questions, (11) Dog whistle bullets - 'For [specific audience]', (12) What to do if / When it's okay to."
  domain: "copywriting"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "FW-FK-079"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Billion Dollar VSL Framework (7 Steps, created with Dean Graziosi): (1) Open â€” cliffhanger story with one-thing promise OR painful question with one-thing promise, (2) Intro â€” 'I'm where you want to be' OR listing of accomplishments, (3) Story â€” hero's journey with 5 substeps, (4) Bridge â€” transition from story to pitch, (5) Pitch â€” here's what I got/what it'll do/what to do next, (6) Bonuses â€” sell the bonus as hard as the main thing, (7) Risk Reversal â€” reassure, reconnect, repitch."
  domain: "video-sales-letters"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "FW-FK-080"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "VSL Story Structure (5 substeps): (1) 'It wasn't always like this' â€” establish current result, (2) 'I had a dream' â€” state the aspiration, (3) Story of struggle â€” tell failures and setbacks, (4) Discovery of the one thing â€” the breakthrough moment, (5) What it's like now â€” paint the after picture hitting emotional triggers (pride, significance, fulfillment, identity)."
  domain: "storytelling"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "FW-FK-081"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "John Carlton's Universal Pitch Framework: (1) Here's what I got, (2) Here's what it will do for you, (3) Here's what I want you to do next. Three things, always works. Learned from John Carlton, changed my life, never strayed from it."
  domain: "copywriting"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "FW-FK-082"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "4-Day Cash Machine Framework: Day 1 email â€” 'Thank you' subject, announce half-off sale with deadline 4 days out, list bonuses. Day 2 â€” 'Don't miss out' same email different opener. Day 3 â€” 'Last day for your [topic]' same pitch. Day 4 at 12:01am â€” 'Final notice' same email, expires tonight."
  domain: "promotion-strategy"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "FW-FK-083"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Double Tap Campaign Architecture: Phase 1 â€” Click sequence (3 days, educational VSL) â†’ if clicked but didn't buy â†’ Countdown sequence (3 days, non-educational VSL, no delayed button). Phase 2 (after Phase 1) â€” Click sequence for non-clickers (non-educational VSL with delayed button) â†’ if clicked but didn't buy â†’ same Countdown sequence."
  domain: "email-marketing"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "FW-FK-084"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Educational VSL Framework (for Double Tap): (1) Explain benefit of video, (2) Seed special offer as open loop, (3) Educational content â€” teach them cool stuff, (4) Transition to value build for gift, (5) Build value for gift, (6) Announce it's free, (7) Explain why it's free, (8) Value build for main thing, (9) Reveal price, (10) Close stack 1, (11) Close stack 2, (12) Reveal bonus and close."
  domain: "video-sales-letters"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "FW-FK-085"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Non-Educational VSL Framework (for Double Tap): (1) Announce gift, (2) Seed benefits of gift, (3) Value build for gift, (4) Announce it's free, (5) Explain why it's free, (6) Value build for main thing, (7) Reveal price, (8) Close stack 1, (9) Close stack 2, (10) Reveal bonus and close. Steps 6-10 are IDENTICAL to the educational version â€” assembly line reuse."
  domain: "video-sales-letters"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "FW-FK-086"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "One-Click Upsell Framework #1 â€” Solve the Problem the Purchase Creates (10 steps): (1) Acknowledge purchase, (2) Present potential positive problem, (3) Prove potential for problem, (4) Prove benefit of solving it, (5) Whet appetite through education, (6) Present solution (your product), (7) Create exclusivity, (8) Give price + show buy button, (9) State benefits while button visible, (10) Create scarcity and close."
  domain: "upsell-strategy"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "FW-FK-087"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "One-Click Upsell Framework #2 â€” Educational Upsell VSL (12 steps): (1) Acknowledge purchase, (2) Present as bonus tutorial, (3) Introduce topic + explain benefit, (4) Debunk common objection, (5) Teach content, (6) Restate benefit, (7) Transition to offer, (8) Introduce product, (9) Build value, (10) Reveal price + show button, (11) State benefits, (12) Scarcity + close."
  domain: "upsell-strategy"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "FW-FK-088"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Close Stacking Technique: After revealing price, take each main component of your offer and demonstrate its value independently against the price. 'Would it be worth $X just to have [component A]?' Repeat for components B, C, D. Then break price into daily rate. Then apples-to-oranges comparison ('What can you buy for $13.05? 1,300 tootsie rolls â€” which would you rather have?')."
  domain: "closing-techniques"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "FW-FK-089"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Professional Services Sales Process Spectrum (Greatest to Least Filtration): (1) Webinar â†’ Application â†’ Approval â†’ Vetting â†’ Phone close. (2) Webinar â†’ Application â†’ Approval â†’ Phone close. (3) Content â†’ Application â†’ Approval â†’ Vetting â†’ Phone close. (4) Content â†’ Application â†’ Approval â†’ Phone close. (5) Content â†’ Self-Schedule â†’ Phone close. (6) Outbound call â†’ Appointment â†’ Phone close."
  domain: "sales-process-design"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "FW-FK-090"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Irresistible Entry Offer (IEO) 5-Phase Framework: (1) I've set aside time to help you with XYZ, (2) Benefits of the conversation â€” A, B, C, (3) Why I'm doing it â€” some people become clients, (4) If you want to become a client, here are my rates, (5) Please rest assured this is NOT a sales pitch + risk reversal ($200 if I waste your time)."
  domain: "high-ticket-sales"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "FW-FK-091"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Click Sequence Email Framework: Subject = get opened. First section = explain benefits of the video. Image = get the click. Body = 'sell' the click with bullet benefits. CTA = 'sell' the click. Send 2 emails per day, 4 hours apart, for 3 days. Only change subject line and first sentence between emails."
  domain: "email-marketing"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "FW-FK-092"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Countdown Sequence Email Framework: Email 1 (45 min after click, unique) = acknowledge + reason why + CTA. Email 2 (4 hours later) = same, change subject only. Day 2 emails = statement of offer + scarcity + benefits. Day 3 = 6 emails (7am, 9am, 1pm, 5pm, 9pm, 11pm) with escalating scarcity. All expire midnight."
  domain: "email-marketing"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "FW-FK-093"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Outbound Call Qualification Script (3-Category Framework): Call leads warmly, confirm email, ask about their business. Then: 'Most people who run ads fall into one of three categories: (1) doing great, (2) think it's working but not sure, (3) it's horrible. Which are you?' Based on answer, attempt to book demo appointment."
  domain: "sales-process-design"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "FW-FK-094"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "VSL Emotional Triggers to Hit in Story: (1) Pride/Accomplishment, (2) Significance, (3) Fulfillment, (4) Emotional Connection/Family, (5) Identity Shift, (6) Health/Vitality. Acknowledge common fears and show you overcame them. If your fear = their fear, you must include it."
  domain: "storytelling"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "FW-FK-095"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Bribe to Trial Framework for Memberships: Give free sunglasses â†’ reward for trying Sunglasses of the Month Club â†’ accept gift, start receiving monthly â†’ if don't want, cancel anytime â†’ keep free pair anyway. Works for any continuity/membership model."
  domain: "offer-strategy"
  source: "OT-W3"

# ===================== L5: METHODOLOGIES =====================

- id: "MET-FK-055"
  layer: L5_METHODOLOGIES
  content: "How to Write a Long-Form Sales Letter (Assembly Line Method): Step 1: Write headline using 4 formulas. Step 2: Write open using Halbert formula. Step 3: Write damaging admission (address skepticism, be repulsive to wrong prospects). Step 4: Write intro to offer ('With that said, let me jump in and show you exactly what you're getting'). Step 5: Expand benefits ('It's about more than just X'). Step 6: Write 20-27 bullets using 12 formulas (3 per formula). Step 7: Transition to bonus stack ('Yes that's a lot of X, but it gets better'). Step 8: Write guarantee (be extreme for low-priced items). Step 9: CTA ('Here's what I want you to do next'). Step 10: Write PS that restates entire offer as mini sales letter."
  domain: "copywriting"
  source: "OT-W3"

- id: "MET-FK-056"
  layer: L5_METHODOLOGIES
  content: "How to Create the Billion Dollar VSL: Step 1: Write open (cliffhanger story + one-thing promise OR painful question + one-thing promise). Step 2: Record intro (I'm where you want to be OR list accomplishments). Step 3: Tell story with 5 parts â€” wasn't always like this, had a dream, struggled, discovered one thing, life now (hit emotional triggers). Step 4: Bridge with 3 sentences ('Solutions are great but without action nothing happens. I have something for you.'). Step 5: Pitch using here's-what-I-got/do-for-you/do-next. Step 6: Stack bonuses selling each as hard as main thing. Step 7: Risk reversal â€” reassure objections, reconnect emotionally, repitch one final time."
  domain: "video-sales-letters"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "MET-FK-057"
  layer: L5_METHODOLOGIES
  content: "How to Run a 4-Day Cash Machine: Day 1: Email entire list with subject 'THANK YOU' (caps). Announce half-off sale with 4-day deadline. List 5 free bonuses. Include 3+ links in email. Day 2: Same email, change first paragraph to 'I wrote you yesterday...' Day 3: Subject 'Last day for your [topic]'. Same pitch, update deadline. Day 4 at 12:01am: Schedule auto-send. Subject 'Final notice.' Same pitch, 'ends tonight.' Sales page = normal sales letter with half-off header, bonus descriptions, and correct date at top."
  domain: "promotion-strategy"
  source: "OT-W4"

- id: "MET-FK-058"
  layer: L5_METHODOLOGIES
  content: "How to Build a Double Tap Campaign: Step 1: Create 3 web pages (educational VSL + delayed button, non-educational VSL + delayed button, non-educational VSL + no delay + countdown timer). Step 2: Write click sequence (6 emails over 3 days driving to educational VSL). Step 3: Write countdown sequence (10 emails over 3 days driving to non-educational VSL without delay).
