  content: "The Off The Cuff Framework for video content: (1) Dumb Stuff â€” warm up, ask 'type yes if you can hear me'; (2) Introduction â€” state today's topic; (3) Offer #1 â€” 'before we get started' framework, say domain, ask viewers to type domain in comments; (4) Elaborate on the point briefly; (5) Offer #2 â€” 'before I forget' framework; (6) Insert relevant story wrapper; (7) Offer #3 â€” 'by the way' framework; (8) Main Point â€” restate your teaching; (9) Q&A â€” answer questions, make offer after each answer."
  domain: "content-creation"
  source: "OT-W2"

- id: "FW-FK-058"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Three Offer Language Patterns: (1) 'By the way' â€” mid-content, casual redirect; (2) 'Before I forget' â€” creates urgency/casualness; (3) 'Before we get started' â€” top of video, first mention. Always say the domain name, always ask viewers to type domain in comments."
  domain: "content-creation"
  source: "OT-W2"

- id: "FW-FK-059"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "The Vortex Engagement Flow: Cold audience sees content â†’ If engaged (watch/click/like/comment/share) â†’ Retarget with 60% content + 40% offers â†’ If they visit website â†’ Retarget multi-channel with 80% offers + 20% content â†’ If they don't click, keep showing 60/40 content/offers."
  domain: "marketing-strategy"
  source: "OT-W1"

- id: "FW-FK-060"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Competitive Research Dossier Framework: For each of top 5 competitors, document: (1) Traffic by countries (find gaps); (2) Demographics (age, gender); (3) Social traffic sources (which platforms); (4) Top content by engagement (80/20 rule); (5) Display ads running and duration; (6) Landing pages and offers; (7) Audience interests; (8) Keywords being bid on."
  domain: "competitive-intelligence"
  source: "OT-W1"

- id: "FW-FK-061"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "IBB Cold-to-Retarget Campaign Structure: Campaign 1 (Cold) = multiple content pieces to strangers â†’ Campaign 2 (Warm) split: 40% budget showing unwatched content + 60% budget showing direct response offer ads with images. The retarget step is where conversions happen."
  domain: "paid-traffic"
  source: "OT-W1"

- id: "FW-FK-062"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Content Idea Generation System â€” 6 sources: (1) Yeah-Buts and What-Ifs from your 3 pillars; (2) Daily life/personal experience/client stories; (3) Social media comments and competitor Q&A; (4) Buzzfeed articles on your topic; (5) Reddit AMAs in your niche; (6) Google 'stories about [topic]' for wrappers."
  domain: "content-strategy"
  source: "OT-W2"

- id: "FW-FK-063"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Weekly Content Planning: Every Monday, review your scratch pad of ideas. Assign one topic per day from your 3 pillars. Each topic = one fear/objection wrapped in a fresh story. You only have to do this once or twice before it becomes habitual."
  domain: "content-strategy"
  source: "OT-W2"

- id: "FW-FK-064"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Facebook Post Structure: Subject line stating video topic â†’ Link to easy-to-remember domain â†’ Video following Off The Cuff framework â†’ In comments: post the link â†’ Ask viewers to type domain in comments for more clickable links and engagement."
  domain: "content-creation"
  source: "OT-W2"

- id: "FW-FK-065"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Multi-Angle Follow-Up Sequence for selling consulting: (1) Webinar pitches application; (2) Series of 3 follow-up videos (~6 min each) re-pitching application; (3) Email with PDF link â€” subject: 'letter from the scene', body: 'not sure I have your address, click to read PDF' â€” PDF is long-form sales letter with application link."
  domain: "sales-funnels"
  source: "OT-W1"

- id: "FW-FK-066"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Simple Sales Letter Structure for strategy sessions: Headline = 'If you want [result], read this.' Body = 'Here's what I got. Here's what it'll do for you. Here's what I need you to do next.' Close = fill in application form."
  domain: "copywriting"
  source: "OT-W1"

- id: "FW-FK-067"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Spy Tools Stack for Competitive Intelligence: (1) SimilarWeb â€” traffic sources, demographics, countries, social breakdown; (2) SEMrush â€” keywords, display ads, landing pages, paid competitors; (3) Social Insider â€” best posts, engagement by type, top videos; (4) Facebook Ads Library â€” current ads, duration running, geographic targeting."
  domain: "competitive-intelligence"
  source: "OT-W1"

# =========================
# L5_METHODOLOGIES
# =========================

- id: "MET-FK-040"
  layer: L5_METHODOLOGIES
  content: "4-Step Competitive Takeover Methodology: Step 1 â€” List top 5 competitors and their websites. Step 2 â€” See what kind of content is working for them (use 80/20 on their videos/posts). Step 3 â€” Know what's going on behind the scenes (buy their stuff, opt into funnels, see upsells). Step 4 â€” Take all data and execute with similar-but-better content, ads, and funnels to the same audiences."
  domain: "competitive-intelligence"
  source: "OT-W1"

- id: "MET-FK-041"
  layer: L5_METHODOLOGIES
  content: "Content Video Creation Methodology (Off The Cuff): (1) Go live on Facebook even if nobody's watching â€” forces you to get good. (2) Follow the Off The Cuff grid: Dumb stuff â†’ Intro â†’ Offer 1 (before we start) â†’ Elaborate â†’ Offer 2 (before I forget) â†’ Story â†’ Offer 3 (by the way) â†’ Main point â†’ Q&A with offers. (3) Make domain easy to remember. (4) Do this every single day."
  domain: "content-creation"
  source: "OT-W2"

- id: "MET-FK-042"
  layer: L5_METHODOLOGIES
  content: "3-Pillar Content Strategy Methodology: (1) Draw a line from 'don't have results' to 'got results.' (2) Map the 3 big things they need to do. (3) For each big thing, list all Yeah-Buts (objections) and What-Ifs (fears). (4) Each objection/fear = one piece of content. (5) Wrap each piece in a story from 6 idea sources. (6) Publish daily, always making offer several times per video."
  domain: "content-strategy"
  source: "OT-W2"

- id: "MET-FK-043"
  layer: L5_METHODOLOGIES
  content: "Competitive Research Deep-Dive Methodology: (1) Enter competitor domain in SimilarWeb â€” note countries, demographics. (2) Check social traffic breakdown (Facebook vs YouTube vs others). (3) In SEMrush, check paid keywords and display ads â€” note ads running 100+ days. (4) In Social Insider, check top 20% of video posts by engagement. (5) In Facebook Ads Library, check active ads and duration. (6) Compile all notes into Competitor Analysis Google Doc with screenshots."
  domain: "competitive-intelligence"
  source: "OT-W1"

- id: "MET-FK-044"
  layer: L5_METHODOLOGIES
  content: "Geo-Fence Targeting for Brick & Mortar: (1) Get MLS listings on market 130-150 days (approaching expiration at 180 days). (2) Geo-target exact home addresses. (3) Show ads: 'Ready to finally sell your house? Call [agent].' (4) Set conversion zones at your own business location. (5) Algorithm tracks: saw ad â†’ visited your location = conversion."
  domain: "geofencing"
  source: "OT-W1"

- id: "MET-FK-045"
  layer: L5_METHODOLOGIES
  content: "Search Term Retargeting for Local Business: (1) Identify high-intent search terms (e.g., 'Ford F-150'). (2) Ad network tags device IDs that searched those terms. (3) Filter by geographic radius from your location. (4) Show banner ads across all websites they visit. (5) Much cheaper than AdWords clicks but captures same intent signals."
  domain: "retargeting"
  source: "OT-W1"

- id: "MET-FK-046"
  layer: L5_METHODOLOGIES
  content: "Addressable List Retargeting Methodology: (1) Get buyer list from list broker (exact data, info USA) filtered by purchase behavior. (2) Upload addresses for geo-fencing each individual household. (3) All mobile devices at those addresses get tagged. (4) When they leave home and connect to other Wi-Fi networks, ads transfer to those devices too. (5) Track conversions when tagged device enters your physical location."
  domain: "geofencing"
  source: "OT-W1"

- id: "MET-FK-047"
  layer: L5_METHODOLOGIES
  content: "IBB for Local Business (Dentist Example): (1) Run content videos with offers: 'Call me if you want your teeth fixed.' (2) Do NOT switch to lead capture forms â€” it changes the natural sales process. (3) Track new patients/calls month-over-month, not just click attribution. (4) Most conversions come from people remembering and typing domain/calling directly, not clicking ads."

## Overview
Closers Into Leaders (CIL) is Cole Gordon's flagship comprehensive sales training program covering the complete lifecycle from individual sales mastery to building and managing high-performing sales teams. The program synthesizes Cole's experience selling $400K+/month as a solo sales rep and scaling multiple sales organizations.

## Structure
| Module | Topic | Files | Key Frameworks |
|--------|-------|-------|----------------|
| 0 | Intro/Onboarding | 7 | DPL x LTV Model, Alignment Triad, Triage Call Flow |
| 1 | Sales Fundamentals | 22 | Seven Belief Ladder, Reverse Selling System, Offer Models, Copy Platform, Inner Game |
| 2 | Client Acquisition | 52 | Pitch Codex, Outbound Messenger, Content Marketing, Pipeline Systems, Two-Step Posts |
| 3 | Closing Mastery | 37 | Belief Blueprint, Discovery Process, Pain/Doubt/Cost/Vision Questions, Transition, Hiring |
| 4 | Objections & Management | 45 | Big Three Objections, Financial Objection Process, Pacing, Big Three Management, Culture |
| 5 | Attraction Prospecting | 16 | APS System, 18 Non-CTA Categories, Other People's Traffic, CTA Offers |
| 6 | Pipeline & Follow-Up | 17 | Re-Offer Technique, Leadership Follow-Up, Two-Step Pipeline, Outbound Calls, Triage Script |
| 7 | Hiring & Team Building | 11 | New Hiring Model (Setter->Closer), Big Three, Scorecards, Onboarding/Ramp-Up |
| 8 | Management & Leadership | 9 | Rut Diagnosis, Data-Driven Decisions, Meeting Cadence, Tracking Systems |
| Calls | Coaching/Group Calls | 118 | Story Selling, Pre-Pitch, Socratic Flow, Offer Creation, Market Sophistication |

### Novos Insights Chave

| Setter-to-Closer Progression | Modelo de carreira: Setter â†’ Junior Closer â†’ Senior Closer â†’ Team Lead â†’ Sales Manager, com KPIs por estagio | Redefine team building â€” nao contrata closers prontos, desenvolve internamente |
